description_long: This fence consists of two distinct sections mounted on a long, narrow base. One section features a series of vertical, thin, light-colored slats closely spaced together, resembling wooden or metal pickets. The other section is composed of solid, dark rectangular panels, possibly made of metal or composite material, with a more opaque and blocky appearance. Both sections are supported by a continuous horizontal rail at the bottom, and the overall structure is low in height and elongated in length. The top edge of the fence is flat and even, with no visible decorative elements or post caps. The design is utilitarian, likely intended for demarcation or light security in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or residential boundaries.
description: A long, low fence with one section of light vertical slats and another of solid dark panels, both mounted on a flat base. The design is simple and functional, with a flat top and no decorative features.
description_view_0: The fence is seen from the side, showing both the light slatted section and the dark solid panel section in full length.
description_view_1: The fence is viewed from the narrow end, making it appear as a thin vertical line with little detail visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, again displaying the full length of both the slatted and solid panel sections, but with the positions reversed compared to view 0.
description_view_3: Another end-on view, similar to view 1, showing the fence as a thin vertical line.
